paper_title: Holocene climate variability in north-eastern Italy: potential influence of the NAO and solar activity recorded by speleothem data

methods.description: This method assesses the influence of the North Atlantic Oscillation (NAO) on winter climate in the cave region by calculating the correlation between the winter NAO index and the amount of winter precipitation and temperature.

steps.description: Precipitation and temperature data are available at monthly resolution since 1921 for nine weather stations in Trentino.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch monthly precipitation and temperature data from Trentino weather stations

steps.description: Instrumental data series were obtained from Meteotrentino and the agro-meteorological network of the Istituto Agrario San Michele all'Adige.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain instrumental data series from Meteotrentino and Istituto Agrario San Michele all'Adige

steps.description: The NAO index data were provided by the Climate Analysis Section, NCAR, Boulder, USA.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch NAO index data from NCAR

steps.description: Calculate the correlation between the winter NAO index and the amount of winter precipitation and temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: perform correlation analysis between NAO index and winter climate data

methods.description: This method involves taking sub-samples for U-series age determination along the growth axis of stalagmite ER 76 and analyzing them using a thermal ionisation mass spectrometer.

steps.description: Sub-samples for U-series age determination were taken along the growth axis of stalagmite ER 76.
steps.searchable_summary: extract sub-samples from stalagmite ER 76 for U-series analysis

steps.description: Analyzed using a Finnigan MAT 262 RPQ thermal ionisation mass spectrometer with a double filament technique.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sub-samples using thermal ionisation mass spectrometer

steps.description: Chemical preparation of the samples was similar as described by Scholz et al. (2004).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples chemically for U-series analysis

steps.description: All ages were calculated using the half-lives reported by Cheng et al. (2000).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate ages using half-lives for U-series

methods.description: This method involves micromilling samples for stable oxygen and carbon isotope analysis and measuring them using an isotope ratio mass spectrometer.

steps.description: Samples for stable oxygen and carbon isotope analysis were micromilled at 100 µm intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: micromill samples for stable isotope analysis

steps.description: All measurements were performed using an online, automated carbonate preparation system linked to a triple collector gas source isotope ratio mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure isotopes using isotope ratio mass spectrometer

steps.description: Long-term precision of the δ 13 C and δ 18 O values, estimated as the 1σ -standard deviation of replicate analyses, is 0.06 and 0.08 ‰, respectively.

methods.method_name: Spectral Analysis of Stable Isotope Signals
methods.description: This method uses the REDFIT software to perform spectral analysis on stable isotope signals from stalagmite ER 76.

steps.description: For spectral analysis of the stable isotope signals of ER 76, we used the software REDFIT 3.8 with specific parameter settings.
steps.searchable_summary: perform spectral analysis using REDFIT software

methods.description: This method involves sampling calcite for 14C analysis, acidifying it, and analyzing it using accelerator mass spectrometry.

steps.description: The 14 C analyses were carried out on ∼ 10 mg of calcite sampled using a dentist drill.
The calcite powder was then acidified in vacuo using HCl.
CO 2 was combusted in the Heidelberg radiocarbon laboratory and analyzed by accelerator mass spectrometry.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze CO2 using accelerator mass spectrometry

methods.description: This method calculates the dead carbon fraction (DCF) using 14C activity data and a Matlab routine.

steps.description: The dcf was calculated using a Matlab routine, which utilises the IntCal04 calibration curve.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate DCF using Matlab and IntCal04 calibration curve
